message:
✨ Add LLM-powered integration test + fully automated PR review (#16)

New pipeline: plan → generate → collect → integration-test → auto-review

Mission Executor (scripts/mission-executor.mjs):
- LLM agent (gpt-4o-mini) acts as a DevOps engineer on a Kind cluster
- Multi-turn: extracts commands from steps, executes, diagnoses failures
- Up to 3 retries per step with LLM-suggested fixes
- Adapts commands for Kind (no LoadBalancer, use NodePort)
- Final verification: LLM confirms pods are running and healthy
- Clean namespace isolation per mission, teardown after test
- Samples 3 missions (prefers cilium/prometheus/envoy, fills with random)

Integration Test job:
- Spins up Kind cluster via helm/kind-action
- Runs mission-executor on sampled missions
- Reports pass/fail/partial per mission
- fail = majority of missions failed on cluster

Auto-Review job (fully automated, no humans):
- Combines quality check verdict + integration test result
- Decision matrix:
  - quality=pass + integ=pass → auto-approve + squash merge
  - quality=pass + integ=warn → approve with warnings, merge
  - quality=pass + integ=fail → draft PR, block merge
  - quality=fail → draft PR, block merge
- Posts structured review comment on PR
- Cleans up branch after merge

Workflow structure:
  plan → generate (batched, max 3 parallel)
    → collect (quality checks: schema, security, links, freshness, index, smoke)
      → integration-test (Kind cluster + LLM executor)
        → auto-review (create PR, review, merge or draft)

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -95,6 +95,19 @@ jobs:
     needs: [plan, generate]
     if: inputs.dry_run != true
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    outputs:
+      published: ${{ steps.report.outputs.published }}
+      drafts: ${{ steps.report.outputs.drafts }}
+      avg_score: ${{ steps.report.outputs.avg_score }}
+      mission_count: ${{ steps.check.outputs.count }}
+      verdict_action: ${{ steps.verdict.outputs.action }}
+      verdict_summary: ${{ steps.verdict.outputs.summary }}
+      schema_result: ${{ steps.schema.outputs.result }}
+      security_result: ${{ steps.security.outputs.result }}
+      links_result: ${{ steps.links.outputs.result }}
+      freshness_result: ${{ steps.freshness.outputs.result }}
+      index_result: ${{ steps.index.outputs.result }}
+      smoketest_result: ${{ steps.smoketest.outputs.result }}
     steps:
       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
       - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
@@ -166,17 +179,37 @@ jobs:
           fi
           echo "count=$COUNT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
 
-      # ── Quality checks ──────────────────────────────────────────
-      - name: 'Quality: Schema validation'
+      # ── Automated Quality Review ─────────────────────────────────
+      # Each check tracks pass/fail. All must pass for auto-merge.
+      # Hard failures (schema, security) → block merge entirely.
+      # Soft warnings (links, freshness) → merge with annotations.
+
+      - name: 'Review: Schema validation'
+        id: schema
         if: steps.check.outputs.count != '0'
         run: |
           echo "## Schema Validation" >> install-report.md
-          FILES=$(find solutions/cncf-install -name '*.json' ! -name '.gitkeep')
-          if [ -n "$FILES" ]; then
-            node scripts/validate-schema.mjs $FILES 2>&1 | tee -a install-report.md
+          FAIL=0
+          for f in $(find solutions/cncf-install -name '*.json' ! -name '.gitkeep'); do
+            if ! node scripts/validate-schema.mjs "$f" >> install-report.md 2>&1; then
+              FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
+              echo "  ❌ $f: INVALID" | tee -a install-report.md
+            else
+              echo "  ✅ $(basename $f): valid" | tee -a install-report.md
+            fi
+          done
+          if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
+            echo "result=fail" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "detail=$FAIL file(s) failed schema validation" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "::error::$FAIL mission(s) failed schema validation — blocking merge"
+          else
+            echo "result=pass" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "detail=All files passed" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "✅ All missions passed schema validation" | tee -a install-report.md
           fi
 
-      - name: 'Quality: Security scan'
+      - name: 'Review: Security scan'
+        id: security
         if: steps.check.outputs.count != '0'
         run: |
           echo "" >> install-report.md
@@ -195,20 +228,27 @@ jobs:
             echo "  $f: $RESULT" | tee -a install-report.md
           done
           if [ "$FINDINGS" -gt 0 ]; then
-            echo "::warning::$FINDINGS mission(s) have security findings"
+            echo "result=fail" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "detail=$FINDINGS file(s) have security findings" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "::error::$FINDINGS mission(s) have security findings — blocking merge"
           else
+            echo "result=pass" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "detail=No findings" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
             echo "✅ All missions passed security scan" | tee -a install-report.md
           fi
 
-      - name: 'Quality: Link validation'
+      - name: 'Review: Link validation'
+        id: links
         if: steps.check.outputs.count != '0'
         run: |
           echo "" >> install-report.md
           echo "## Link Validation" >> install-report.md
           BROKEN=0
+          CHECKED=0
           for f in $(find solutions/cncf-install -name '*.json' ! -name '.gitkeep' ! -name '*.draft.json'); do
             REPO_URL=$(node -e "const d=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('$f','utf-8')); console.log(d.metadata?.sourceUrls?.repo || '')")
             if [ -n "$REPO_URL" ]; then
+              CHECKED=$((CHECKED + 1))
               STATUS=$(curl -sL -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 10 "$REPO_URL" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
               if [ "$STATUS" -ge 400 ] || [ "$STATUS" = "000" ]; then
                 echo "  ⚠️ $f: $REPO_URL → HTTP $STATUS" | tee -a install-report.md
@@ -217,25 +257,32 @@ jobs:
             fi
           done
           if [ "$BROKEN" -gt 0 ]; then
+            echo "result=warn" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "detail=$BROKEN/$CHECKED URLs broken" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
             echo "::warning::$BROKEN mission(s) have broken source URLs"
           else
-            echo "✅ All source URLs valid" | tee -a install-report.md
+            echo "result=pass" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "detail=$CHECKED URLs checked, all valid" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "✅ All source URLs valid ($CHECKED checked)" | tee -a install-report.md
           fi
 
-      - name: 'Quality: Version freshness'
+      - name: 'Review: Version freshness'
+        id: freshness
         if: steps.check.outputs.count != '0'
         env:
           GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
         run: |
           echo "" >> install-report.md
           echo "## Version Freshness" >> install-report.md
           STALE=0
+          CHECKED=0
           for f in $(find solutions/cncf-install -name '*.json' ! -name '.gitkeep' ! -name '*.draft.json'); do
             read -r REPO VERSION <<< $(node -e "
               const d=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('$f','utf-8'));
               console.log((d.metadata?.sourceUrls?.repo || '').replace('https://github.com/','') + ' ' + (d.metadata?.projectVersion || 'latest'))
             ")
             if [ "$VERSION" != "latest" ] && [ -n "$REPO" ]; then
+              CHECKED=$((CHECKED + 1))
               LATEST=$(curl -sL -H "Authorization: Bearer $GH_TOKEN" "https://api.github.com/repos/$REPO/releases/latest" 2>/dev/null | node -e "let d='';process.stdin.on('data',c=>d+=c);process.stdin.on('end',()=>{try{console.log(JSON.parse(d).tag_name||'')}catch{console.log('')}})" 2>/dev/null)
               if [ -n "$LATEST" ] && [ "$LATEST" != "$VERSION" ]; then
                 echo "  ⚠️ $(basename $f): has $VERSION, latest is $LATEST" | tee -a install-report.md
@@ -244,31 +291,45 @@ jobs:
             fi
           done
           if [ "$STALE" -gt 0 ]; then
+            echo "result=warn" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "detail=$STALE/$CHECKED outdated" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
             echo "::warning::$STALE mission(s) reference outdated versions"
           else
-            echo "✅ All versions current" | tee -a install-report.md
+            echo "result=pass" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "detail=All $CHECKED versions current" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "✅ All versions current ($CHECKED checked)" | tee -a install-report.md
           fi
 
-      - name: 'Quality: Rebuild index'
+      - name: 'Review: Rebuild index'
+        id: index
         if: steps.check.outputs.count != '0'
         run: |
-          node scripts/build-index.mjs
-          echo "" >> install-report.md
-          INDEXED=$(node -e "const d=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('solutions/index.json','utf-8')); console.log(d.count)")
-          echo "## Index: $INDEXED missions indexed" | tee -a install-report.md
+          if node scripts/build-index.mjs 2>&1; then
+            INDEXED=$(node -e "const d=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('solutions/index.json','utf-8')); console.log(d.count)")
+            echo "" >> install-report.md
+            echo "## Index: $INDEXED missions indexed ✅" | tee -a install-report.md
+            echo "result=pass" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "detail=$INDEXED missions indexed" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+          else
+            echo "result=fail" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "detail=Index build failed" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "::error::Index rebuild failed — blocking merge"
+          fi
 
-      - name: 'Quality: Command syntax smoke test'
+      - name: 'Review: Command smoke test'
+        id: smoketest
         if: steps.check.outputs.count != '0'
         run: |
           echo "" >> install-report.md
           echo "## Command Smoke Test" >> install-report.md
           BAD=0
-          for f in $(find solutions/cncf-install -name '*.json' ! -name '.gitkeep' ! -name '*.draft.json' | shuf | head -5); do
-            # Check that helm/kubectl commands have valid structure
+          TOTAL=0
+          for f in $(find solutions/cncf-install -name '*.json' ! -name '.gitkeep' ! -name '*.draft.json'); do
+            TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
             CMDS=$(node -e "
               const d=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('$f','utf-8'));
               const steps = d.mission?.steps || [];
-              const cmds = steps.flatMap(s => (s.description||'').match(/(?:helm|kubectl|docker)\s+\w+[^\n\`]*/g) || []);
+              const cmds = steps.flatMap(s => (s.description||'').match(/(?:helm|kubectl|docker|kustomize|operator-sdk)\s+\w+[^\n\`]*/g) || []);
               cmds.forEach(c => console.log(c.trim().slice(0, 120)));
             ")
             if [ -z "$CMDS" ]; then
@@ -279,48 +340,450 @@ jobs:
             fi
           done
           if [ "$BAD" -gt 0 ]; then
-            echo "::warning::$BAD sampled mission(s) have no CLI commands"
+            echo "result=warn" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "detail=$BAD/$TOTAL missions missing CLI commands" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "::warning::$BAD mission(s) have no CLI commands"
+          else
+            echo "result=pass" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "detail=All $TOTAL missions have CLI commands" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+          fi
+
+      # ── Automated Review Decision ──────────────────────────────
+      - name: 'Review: Render verdict'
+        id: verdict
+        if: steps.check.outputs.count != '0'
+        run: |
+          echo "" >> install-report.md
+          echo "---" >> install-report.md
+          echo "## 🤖 Automated Review Verdict" >> install-report.md
+          echo "" >> install-report.md
+          echo "| Check | Result | Detail |" >> install-report.md
+          echo "|-------|--------|--------|" >> install-report.md
+
+          HARD_FAIL=0
+          WARNINGS=0
+
+          # Hard gates — any failure blocks merge
+          for CHECK in schema security index; do
+            RESULT="${{ steps.schema.outputs.result }}"
+            DETAIL="${{ steps.schema.outputs.detail }}"
+            case "$CHECK" in
+              security) RESULT="${{ steps.security.outputs.result }}"; DETAIL="${{ steps.security.outputs.detail }}" ;;
+              index) RESULT="${{ steps.index.outputs.result }}"; DETAIL="${{ steps.index.outputs.detail }}" ;;
+            esac
+            ICON="✅"
+            if [ "$RESULT" = "fail" ]; then
+              ICON="❌"
+              HARD_FAIL=$((HARD_FAIL + 1))
+            fi
+            echo "| $CHECK | $ICON $RESULT | $DETAIL |" >> install-report.md
+          done
+
+          # Soft gates — warnings don't block merge
+          for CHECK in links freshness smoketest; do
+            RESULT="${{ steps.links.outputs.result }}"
+            DETAIL="${{ steps.links.outputs.detail }}"
+            case "$CHECK" in
+              freshness) RESULT="${{ steps.freshness.outputs.result }}"; DETAIL="${{ steps.freshness.outputs.detail }}" ;;
+              smoketest) RESULT="${{ steps.smoketest.outputs.result }}"; DETAIL="${{ steps.smoketest.outputs.detail }}" ;;
+            esac
+            ICON="✅"
+            if [ "$RESULT" = "warn" ]; then
+              ICON="⚠️"
+              WARNINGS=$((WARNINGS + 1))
+            elif [ "$RESULT" = "fail" ]; then
+              ICON="❌"
+              HARD_FAIL=$((HARD_FAIL + 1))
+            fi
+            echo "| $CHECK | $ICON $RESULT | $DETAIL |" >> install-report.md
+          done
+
+          # Batch quality score check
+          AVG="${{ steps.report.outputs.avg_score }}"
+          if [ "${AVG:-0}" -lt 50 ]; then
+            echo "| batch_score | ❌ fail | avg=$AVG (<50) |" >> install-report.md
+            HARD_FAIL=$((HARD_FAIL + 1))
+          else
+            echo "| batch_score | ✅ pass | avg=$AVG |" >> install-report.md
+          fi
+
+          echo "" >> install-report.md
+
+          # Final verdict
+          if [ "$HARD_FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
+            echo "**VERDICT: ❌ BLOCKED** — $HARD_FAIL hard failure(s). PR created as draft for investigation." >> install-report.md
+            echo "action=draft" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "verdict=blocked" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "summary=$HARD_FAIL hard failure(s), $WARNINGS warning(s)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+          elif [ "$WARNINGS" -gt 0 ]; then
+            echo "**VERDICT: ✅ APPROVED WITH WARNINGS** — $WARNINGS soft warning(s). Auto-merging." >> install-report.md
+            echo "action=merge" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "verdict=approved_with_warnings" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "summary=Approved with $WARNINGS warning(s)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+          else
+            echo "**VERDICT: ✅ APPROVED** — All checks passed. Auto-merging." >> install-report.md
+            echo "action=merge" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "verdict=approved" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "summary=All checks passed" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
           fi
 
       - name: Post generation summary
         if: steps.check.outputs.count != '0'
         run: |
-          echo "### Generation Summary" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "### 🤖 Automated Review Summary" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
           echo "| Metric | Value |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
           echo "|--------|-------|" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
           echo "| Published | ${{ steps.report.outputs.published }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
           echo "| Drafts | ${{ steps.report.outputs.drafts }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
           echo "| Average Score | ${{ steps.report.outputs.avg_score }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
-          echo "| Draft PR | ${{ steps.report.outputs.draft_pr }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "| Schema | ${{ steps.schema.outputs.result }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "| Security | ${{ steps.security.outputs.result }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "| Links | ${{ steps.links.outputs.result }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "| Freshness | ${{ steps.freshness.outputs.result }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "| Index | ${{ steps.index.outputs.result }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "| Smoke Test | ${{ steps.smoketest.outputs.result }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "| **Verdict** | **${{ steps.verdict.outputs.verdict }}** |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
 
-      # ── Create PR ───────────────────────────────────────────────
-      - name: Create PR
+      # Upload missions + report for downstream jobs
+      - name: Upload reviewed missions
         if: steps.check.outputs.count != '0'
+        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: reviewed-missions
+          path: |
+            solutions/
+            install-report.md
+
+  # ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+  # Integration Test: LLM-powered mission execution on Kind cluster
+  # Samples 3 missions and runs them with an AI agent that executes
+  # each step, diagnoses failures, and retries — full end-to-end.
+  # ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+  integration-test:
+    needs: [plan, collect]
+    if: |
+      inputs.dry_run != true &&
+      needs.collect.outputs.verdict_action == 'merge' &&
+      needs.collect.outputs.mission_count != '0'
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 30
+    outputs:
+      result: ${{ steps.execute.outputs.result }}
+      passed: ${{ steps.execute.outputs.passed }}
+      failed: ${{ steps.execute.outputs.failed }}
+      total: ${{ steps.execute.outputs.total }}
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: '20'
+      - name: Install dependencies
+        run: cd scripts && npm install --no-audit
+
+      - name: Download reviewed missions
+        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: reviewed-missions
+
+      - name: Set up Kind cluster
+        uses: helm/kind-action@v1
+        with:
+          cluster_name: mission-test
+          wait: 120s
+
+      - name: Install Helm
+        uses: azure/setup-helm@v4
+
+      - name: Verify cluster
+        run: |
+          kubectl cluster-info
+          kubectl get nodes
+          echo "Cluster ready for mission execution"
+
+      - name: Sample missions for testing
+        id: sample
+        run: |
+          # Pick up to 3 published (non-draft) missions, preferring popular projects
+          PREFERRED="cilium prometheus envoy"
+          SELECTED=""
+          for proj in $PREFERRED; do
+            FILE=$(find solutions/cncf-install -name "${proj}.json" ! -name '*.draft.json' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
+            if [ -n "$FILE" ]; then
+              SELECTED="$SELECTED $FILE"
+            fi
+          done
+          # Fill remaining slots with random missions
+          REMAINING=$((3 - $(echo $SELECTED | wc -w | tr -d ' ')))
+          if [ "$REMAINING" -gt 0 ]; then
+            RANDOM_FILES=$(find solutions/cncf-install -name '*.json' ! -name '*.draft.json' ! -name '.gitkeep' 2>/dev/null | shuf | head -$REMAINING)
+            SELECTED="$SELECTED $RANDOM_FILES"
+          fi
+          SELECTED=$(echo $SELECTED | xargs)
+          echo "files=$SELECTED" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+          echo "count=$(echo $SELECTED | wc -w | tr -d ' ')" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+          echo "Selected missions for integration test:"
+          for f in $SELECTED; do echo "  - $f"; done
+
+      - name: Execute missions on Kind
+        id: execute
+        if: steps.sample.outputs.count != '0'
+        env:
+          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+          REPORT_PATH: mission-execution-report.json
+          MISSION_TIMEOUT_MS: '300000'
+          STEP_TIMEOUT_MS: '120000'
+          MAX_RETRIES: '3'
+        run: |
+          set +e
+          node scripts/mission-executor.mjs ${{ steps.sample.outputs.files }}
+          EXIT_CODE=$?
+          set -e
+
+          # Parse results
+          if [ -f mission-execution-report.json ]; then
+            PASSED=$(node -e "const r=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('mission-execution-report.json','utf-8')); console.log(r.summary.passed)")
+            FAILED=$(node -e "const r=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('mission-execution-report.json','utf-8')); console.log(r.summary.failed)")
+            TOTAL=$(node -e "const r=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('mission-execution-report.json','utf-8')); console.log(r.summary.total)")
+            PARTIAL=$(node -e "const r=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('mission-execution-report.json','utf-8')); console.log(r.summary.partial)")
+            echo "passed=$PASSED" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "failed=$FAILED" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "total=$TOTAL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+
+            if [ "$FAILED" -gt "$(( TOTAL / 2 ))" ]; then
+              echo "result=fail" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+              echo "::warning::Integration test: $FAILED/$TOTAL missions failed"
+            elif [ "$FAILED" -gt 0 ]; then
+              echo "result=warn" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+              echo "::warning::Integration test: $FAILED/$TOTAL missions failed (below threshold)"
+            else
+              echo "result=pass" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            fi
+          else
+            echo "result=error" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "passed=0" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "failed=0" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "total=0" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+          fi
+
+      - name: Upload execution report
+        if: always()
+        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: integration-test-report
+          path: mission-execution-report.json
+          if-no-files-found: ignore
+
+      - name: Post Kind test summary
+        if: always()
+        run: |
+          echo "### 🧪 Integration Test (Kind Cluster)" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          if [ -f mission-execution-report.json ]; then
+            echo "| Mission | Verdict | Duration |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+            echo "|---------|---------|----------|" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+            node -e "
+              const r = JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('mission-execution-report.json','utf-8'));
+              for (const m of r.results) {
+                const icon = {pass:'✅',partial:'⚠️',pass_unverified:'⚠️',fail:'❌',error:'💥'}[m.verdict]||'❓';
+                const dur = m.duration_ms ? (m.duration_ms/1000).toFixed(1)+'s' : '-';
+                console.log('| ' + (m.mission||m.file).slice(0,50) + ' | ' + icon + ' ' + m.verdict + ' | ' + dur + ' |');
+              }
+            " >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+            echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+            echo "**Result:** ${{ steps.execute.outputs.result }} (${{ steps.execute.outputs.passed }}/${{ steps.execute.outputs.total }} passed)" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          else
+            echo "No execution report generated." >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          fi
+
+  # ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+  # Auto-Review: Create PR, post review, and merge/draft based on
+  # quality checks + integration test results. No human needed.
+  # ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+  auto-review:
+    needs: [plan, collect, integration-test]
+    if: |
+      always() &&
+      inputs.dry_run != true &&
+      needs.collect.outputs.mission_count != '0'
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Download reviewed missions
+        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: reviewed-missions
+
+      - name: Download integration test report
+        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: integration-test-report
+          path: .
+        continue-on-error: true
+
+      - name: Determine final action
+        id: final
+        run: |
+          COLLECT_VERDICT="${{ needs.collect.outputs.verdict_action }}"
+          INTEG_RESULT="${{ needs.integration-test.outputs.result }}"
+          INTEG_PASSED="${{ needs.integration-test.outputs.passed || '0' }}"
+          INTEG_FAILED="${{ needs.integration-test.outputs.failed || '0' }}"
+          INTEG_TOTAL="${{ needs.integration-test.outputs.total || '0' }}"
+
+          echo "" >> install-report.md
+          echo "---" >> install-report.md
+          echo "## 🧪 Integration Test Results" >> install-report.md
+
+          if [ -f mission-execution-report.json ]; then
+            echo "" >> install-report.md
+            echo "| Mission | Verdict | Duration |" >> install-report.md
+            echo "|---------|---------|----------|" >> install-report.md
+            node -e "
+              const r = JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('mission-execution-report.json','utf-8'));
+              for (const m of r.results) {
+                const icon = {pass:'✅',partial:'⚠️',pass_unverified:'⚠️',fail:'❌',error:'💥'}[m.verdict]||'❓';
+                const dur = m.duration_ms ? (m.duration_ms/1000).toFixed(1)+'s' : '-';
+                console.log('| ' + (m.mission||m.file).slice(0,60) + ' | ' + icon + ' ' + m.verdict + ' | ' + dur + ' |');
+              }
+            " >> install-report.md
+            echo "" >> install-report.md
+            echo "**Passed:** $INTEG_PASSED/$INTEG_TOTAL | **Result:** $INTEG_RESULT" >> install-report.md
+          else
+            echo "Integration test did not produce a report (may have been skipped)." >> install-report.md
+            INTEG_RESULT="skipped"
+          fi
+
+          # Final decision matrix:
+          # collect=merge + integ=pass/warn/skipped → merge
+          # collect=merge + integ=fail → draft (integ failures block)
+          # collect=draft → draft (quality failures block)
+          echo "" >> install-report.md

+
+          if [ "$COLLECT_VERDICT" = "draft" ]; then

+            echo "**❌ BLOCKED** — Quality checks failed. Draft PR for investigation." >> install-report.md
+          elif [ "$INTEG_RESULT" = "fail" ]; then

+            echo "**❌ BLOCKED** — Integration test failed ($INTEG_FAILED/$INTEG_TOTAL). Draft PR for investigation." >> install-report.md
+          elif [ "$INTEG_RESULT" = "warn" ]; then
+            echo "action=merge" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "reason=Quality passed, integration partial ($INTEG_PASSED/$INTEG_TOTAL)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "**✅ APPROVED WITH WARNINGS** — Quality passed, integration partial ($INTEG_PASSED/$INTEG_TOTAL). Auto-merging." >> install-report.md
+          else
+            echo "action=merge" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "reason=All checks passed" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "**✅ APPROVED** — Quality checks + integration test passed. Auto-merging." >> install-report.md

+
+      - name: Create PR
+        id: pr
         env:
           GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
         run: |
           git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
           git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

           git add solutions/cncf-install/ solutions/index.json
           git commit -s -m "✨ Auto-generate CNCF install missions ($(date +%Y-%m-%d))
 
           Generated install & configure missions across ${{ needs.plan.outputs.batch_count }} batches.
-          Published: ${{ steps.report.outputs.published }}, Drafts: ${{ steps.report.outputs.drafts }}

+          Average quality score: ${{ needs.collect.outputs.avg_score }}

             DRAFT_FLAG="--draft"

-          gh pr create \

+            $DRAFT_FLAG)
+
+          PR_NUM=$(echo "$PR_URL" | grep -oP '\d+$')
+          echo "pr_number=$PR_NUM" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+          echo "pr_url=$PR_URL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+          echo "branch=$BRANCH" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+
+      - name: 'Auto-review: Post review'
+        if: steps.pr.outputs.pr_number != ''
+        env:
+          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+        run: |
+          ACTION="${{ steps.final.outputs.action }}"
+          REASON="${{ steps.final.outputs.reason }}"
+
+          REVIEW_BODY="## 🤖 Automated Mission Review
+
+          **Final Verdict: $ACTION**
+          **Reason:** $REASON
+
+          ### Quality Checks
+          | Check | Result |
+          |-------|--------|
+          | Schema | ${{ needs.collect.outputs.schema_result }} |
+          | Security | ${{ needs.collect.outputs.security_result }} |
+          | Links | ${{ needs.collect.outputs.links_result }} |
+          | Freshness | ${{ needs.collect.outputs.freshness_result }} |
+          | Index | ${{ needs.collect.outputs.index_result }} |
+          | Smoke Test | ${{ needs.collect.outputs.smoketest_result }} |
+          | Batch Score | avg=${{ needs.collect.outputs.avg_score }} |
+
+          ### Integration Test (Kind)

+          | Result | ${{ needs.integration-test.outputs.result || 'skipped' }} |
+          | Passed | ${{ needs.integration-test.outputs.passed || '0' }}/${{ needs.integration-test.outputs.total || '0' }} |
+
+          **Stats:** ${{ needs.collect.outputs.published }} published, ${{ needs.collect.outputs.drafts }} drafts
+
+          ---

+
+          if [ "$ACTION" = "merge" ]; then

+          else
+            gh pr review ${{ steps.pr.outputs.pr_number }} --request-changes --body "$REVIEW_BODY"

+          gh pr merge ${{ steps.pr.outputs.pr_number }} --squash --admin \
+            --subject "✨ CNCF install missions (auto-reviewed) $(date +%Y-%m-%d)" \

+          echo "| **Final Action** | **${{ steps.final.outputs.action }}** |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

@@ -0,0 +1,455 @@
+/**
+ * Mission Executor — LLM-powered integration test for install missions.
+ *
+ * Reads a mission JSON file, spins up against a Kind cluster, and uses an LLM
+ * agent in a multi-turn loop to:
+ *   1. Parse commands from each step's description
+ *   2. Execute them against the cluster
+ *   3. Diagnose failures and retry (max 3 attempts per step)
+ *   4. Run verification steps and confirm the install worked
+ *   5. Clean up (delete namespace)
+ *
+ * Usage:
+ *   GITHUB_TOKEN=xxx node scripts/mission-executor.mjs <mission.json> [mission2.json ...]
+ *
+ * Environment:
+ *   GITHUB_TOKEN     — GitHub Models API auth (required)
+ *   KUBECONFIG       — path to kubeconfig (default: ~/.kube/config)
+ *   LLM_MODEL        — model to use (default: gpt-4o-mini)
+ *   MAX_RETRIES      — retries per step (default: 3)
+ *   STEP_TIMEOUT_MS  — timeout per command (default: 120000)
+ *   MISSION_TIMEOUT_MS — timeout per mission (default: 300000)
+ *   DRY_RUN          — if "true", print commands but don't execute
+ */
+
+import { readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'fs'
+import { execSync, spawn } from 'child_process'
+
+const LLM_ENDPOINT = process.env.LLM_ENDPOINT || 'https://models.inference.ai.azure.com/chat/completions'
+const LLM_MODEL = process.env.LLM_MODEL || 'gpt-4o-mini'
+const MAX_RETRIES = parseInt(process.env.MAX_RETRIES || '3', 10)
+const STEP_TIMEOUT_MS = parseInt(process.env.STEP_TIMEOUT_MS || '120000', 10)
+const MISSION_TIMEOUT_MS = parseInt(process.env.MISSION_TIMEOUT_MS || '300000', 10)
+const DRY_RUN = process.env.DRY_RUN === 'true'
+
+function getToken() {
+  return process.env.LLM_TOKEN || process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN
+}
+
+// ── Shell execution ─────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+function execCommand(cmd, timeoutMs = STEP_TIMEOUT_MS) {
+  try {
+    const output = execSync(cmd, {
+      encoding: 'utf-8',
+      timeout: timeoutMs,
+      stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
+      env: { ...process.env, TERM: 'dumb' },
+    })
+    return { success: true, output: output.trim(), exitCode: 0 }
+  } catch (err) {
+    return {
+      success: false,
+      output: (err.stdout || '') + '\n' + (err.stderr || ''),
+      exitCode: err.status || 1,
+      error: err.message,
+    }
+  }
+}
+
+// ── LLM conversation ───────────────────────────────────────────
+
+const SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are a Kubernetes DevOps engineer executing installation missions on a Kind cluster.
+You receive mission steps and execute them one at a time. For each step:
+
+1. Extract the shell commands from the step description (they are usually in code blocks).
+2. Adapt commands for a Kind cluster if needed (e.g. no LoadBalancer, use NodePort or port-forward).
+3. If a command fails, diagnose the error and suggest a fix.
+4. After verification steps, confirm whether the installation succeeded.
+
+RULES:
+- Only output valid JSON responses.
+- Commands must be safe for a test cluster (no production destructive operations).
+- If a Helm repo add fails, try without the repo and use OCI or direct URL.
+- Wait for pods with: kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=X --timeout=120s
+- If a namespace doesn't exist, create it.
+- Never use 'sudo' or install system packages.
+- If you need to adapt a command, explain what you changed and why.
+
+Respond in JSON:
+{
+  "commands": ["cmd1", "cmd2", ...],
+  "reasoning": "why these commands",
+  "adaptations": "what was changed for Kind cluster, if anything"
+}
+
+When diagnosing failures, respond in JSON:
+{
+  "diagnosis": "what went wrong",
+  "fix_commands": ["fixed_cmd1", ...],
+  "skip": false
+}
+Set "skip": true only if the step is genuinely optional (e.g. external DNS, cloud-specific LB).`
+
+async function llmChat(messages) {
+  const token = getToken()
+  if (!token) throw new Error('No GITHUB_TOKEN set for LLM API')
+
+  const resp = await fetch(LLM_ENDPOINT, {
+    method: 'POST',
+    headers: {
+      'Authorization': `Bearer ${token}`,
+      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
+    },
+    body: JSON.stringify({
+      model: LLM_MODEL,
+      messages,
+      temperature: 0.2,
+      max_tokens: 1500,
+      response_format: { type: 'json_object' },
+    }),
+    signal: AbortSignal.timeout(30000),
+  })
+
+  if (!resp.ok) {
+    const body = await resp.text()
+    throw new Error(`LLM API error ${resp.status}: ${body.slice(0, 200)}`)
+  }
+
+  const data = await resp.json()
+  const content = data.choices?.[0]?.message?.content
+  if (!content) throw new Error('Empty LLM response')
+
+  return JSON.parse(content)
+}
+
+// ── Mission execution ──────────────────────────────────────────
+
+async function executeStep(step, stepIndex, missionContext, conversationHistory) {
+  const result = {
+    step: stepIndex + 1,
+    title: step.title,
+    status: 'pending',
+    commands_run: [],
+    attempts: 0,
+    output: '',
+  }
+
+  // Ask LLM to extract commands from the step
+  const extractMsg = {
+    role: 'user',
+    content: JSON.stringify({
+      action: 'extract_commands',
+      step_number: stepIndex + 1,
+      step_title: step.title,
+      step_description: step.description,
+      mission_context: missionContext,
+    }),
+  }
+
+  conversationHistory.push(extractMsg)
+
+  let llmResponse
+  try {
+    llmResponse = await llmChat([
+      { role: 'system', content: SYSTEM_PROMPT },
+      ...conversationHistory,
+    ])
+  } catch (err) {
+    result.status = 'error'
+    result.output = `LLM extraction failed: ${err.message}`
+    return result
+  }
+
+  conversationHistory.push({ role: 'assistant', content: JSON.stringify(llmResponse) })
+
+  const commands = llmResponse.commands || []
+  if (commands.length === 0) {
+    // LLM found no actionable commands — might be a description-only step
+    result.status = 'skipped'
+    result.output = 'No actionable commands in this step'
+    return result
+  }
+
+  console.log(`    Step ${stepIndex + 1}: ${step.title}`)
+  if (llmResponse.adaptations) {
+    console.log(`    Adaptations: ${llmResponse.adaptations}`)
+  }
+
+  // Execute commands with retry loop
+  for (const cmd of commands) {
+    let lastResult = null
+
+    for (let attempt = 0; attempt < MAX_RETRIES; attempt++) {
+      result.attempts = attempt + 1
+      const cmdToRun = attempt === 0 ? cmd : lastResult?.fixCmd || cmd
+
+      console.log(`      [attempt ${attempt + 1}] ${cmdToRun.slice(0, 120)}`)
+      result.commands_run.push(cmdToRun)
+
+      if (DRY_RUN) {
+        console.log(`      [dry-run] would execute: ${cmdToRun}`)
+        lastResult = { success: true, output: '[dry-run]' }
+        break
+      }
+
+      const execResult = execCommand(cmdToRun)
+
+      if (execResult.success) {
+        console.log(`      ✅ success`)
+        lastResult = execResult
+        break
+      }
+
+      console.log(`      ❌ failed (exit ${execResult.exitCode})`)
+
+      // Ask LLM to diagnose and suggest fix
+      const diagMsg = {
+        role: 'user',
+        content: JSON.stringify({
+          action: 'diagnose_failure',
+          command: cmdToRun,
+          exit_code: execResult.exitCode,
+          error_output: execResult.output.slice(-2000),
+          attempt: attempt + 1,
+          max_attempts: MAX_RETRIES,
+        }),
+      }
+
+      conversationHistory.push(diagMsg)
+
+      try {
+        const diagResponse = await llmChat([
+          { role: 'system', content: SYSTEM_PROMPT },
+          ...conversationHistory.slice(-10), // keep context window small
+        ])
+
+        conversationHistory.push({ role: 'assistant', content: JSON.stringify(diagResponse) })
+
+        if (diagResponse.skip) {
+          console.log(`      ⏭️  LLM says skip: ${diagResponse.diagnosis}`)
+          lastResult = { success: true, output: `Skipped: ${diagResponse.diagnosis}`, skipped: true }
+          break
+        }
+
+        if (diagResponse.fix_commands?.length > 0) {
+          console.log(`      🔧 LLM fix: ${diagResponse.diagnosis}`)
+          // Execute fix commands first, then retry
+          for (const fixCmd of diagResponse.fix_commands) {
+            console.log(`      [fix] ${fixCmd.slice(0, 120)}`)
+            result.commands_run.push(fixCmd)
+            if (!DRY_RUN) execCommand(fixCmd)
+          }
+          lastResult = { ...execResult, fixCmd: diagResponse.fix_commands[diagResponse.fix_commands.length - 1] }
+        } else {
+          lastResult = execResult
+        }
+      } catch (diagErr) {
+        console.log(`      ⚠️ LLM diagnosis failed: ${diagErr.message}`)
+        lastResult = execResult
+      }
+    }
+
+    if (!lastResult?.success && !lastResult?.skipped) {
+      result.status = 'failed'
+      result.output = lastResult?.output?.slice(-500) || 'Command failed after retries'
+      return result
+    }
+  }
+
+  result.status = 'passed'
+  result.output = 'All commands succeeded'
+  return result
+}
+
+async function executeMission(missionPath) {
+  const startTime = Date.now()
+  const raw = readFileSync(missionPath, 'utf-8')
+  const mission = JSON.parse(raw)
+
+  const title = mission.mission?.title || mission.name || missionPath
+  const steps = mission.mission?.steps || []
+  const namespace = `test-${mission.name || 'mission'}-${Date.now() % 10000}`
+
+  console.log(`\n${'═'.repeat(70)}`)
+  console.log(`  🚀 Executing: ${title}`)
+  console.log(`  📁 File: ${missionPath}`)
+  console.log(`  📦 Namespace: ${namespace}`)
+  console.log(`  📋 Steps: ${steps.length}`)
+  console.log(`${'═'.repeat(70)}`)
+
+  const report = {
+    mission: title,
+    file: missionPath,
+    namespace,
+    steps: [],
+    verdict: 'pending',
+    duration_ms: 0,
+    error: null,
+  }
+
+  // Create isolated namespace
+  if (!DRY_RUN) {
+    execCommand(`kubectl create namespace ${namespace} --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -`)
+  }
+
+  const missionContext = {
+    namespace,
+    cluster_type: 'kind',
+    kubernetes_version: execCommand('kubectl version --client -o json 2>/dev/null | grep gitVersion || echo "unknown"').output,
+    available_tools: ['kubectl', 'helm', 'curl'],
+    note: 'This is a Kind cluster — no LoadBalancer, no cloud storage. Use NodePort or port-forward.',
+  }
+
+  const conversationHistory = []
+
+  let allPassed = true
+  let verificationPassed = false
+
+  for (let i = 0; i < steps.length; i++) {
+    // Check mission timeout
+    if (Date.now() - startTime > MISSION_TIMEOUT_MS) {
+      console.log(`  ⏰ Mission timeout (${MISSION_TIMEOUT_MS / 1000}s) — aborting remaining steps`)
+      report.steps.push({ step: i + 1, title: steps[i].title, status: 'timeout' })
+      break
+    }
+
+    const stepResult = await executeStep(steps[i], i, missionContext, conversationHistory)
+    report.steps.push(stepResult)
+
+    if (stepResult.status === 'failed') {
+      allPassed = false
+      console.log(`    ❌ Step ${i + 1} failed — continuing to see what else works`)
+    }
+
+    // Track if verification step passed
+    const desc = (steps[i].description || '').toLowerCase()
+    if (desc.includes('verify') || desc.includes('health') || desc.includes('check') || steps[i].title?.toLowerCase().includes('verify')) {
+      if (stepResult.status === 'passed') verificationPassed = true
+    }
+  }
+
+  // Final verification: ask LLM to confirm overall status
+  console.log(`\n  🔍 Final verification...`)
+  if (!DRY_RUN) {
+    const podsResult = execCommand(`kubectl get pods -n ${namespace} --no-headers 2>/dev/null || echo "no pods"`)
+    const svcsResult = execCommand(`kubectl get svc -n ${namespace} --no-headers 2>/dev/null || echo "no services"`)
+
+    conversationHistory.push({
+      role: 'user',
+      content: JSON.stringify({
+        action: 'final_verification',
+        pods: podsResult.output.slice(0, 1000),
+        services: svcsResult.output.slice(0, 500),
+        step_results: report.steps.map(s => ({ step: s.step, title: s.title, status: s.status })),
+      }),
+    })
+
+    try {
+      const verifyResponse = await llmChat([
+        { role: 'system', content: SYSTEM_PROMPT + '\nFor final_verification, respond: {"installed": true/false, "healthy": true/false, "summary": "..."}' },
+        ...conversationHistory.slice(-6),
+      ])
+
+      if (verifyResponse.installed && verifyResponse.healthy) {
+        console.log(`  ✅ LLM confirms: installed and healthy`)
+        verificationPassed = true
+      } else {
+        console.log(`  ⚠️ LLM says: ${verifyResponse.summary || 'not fully installed'}`)
+      }
+    } catch {
+      console.log(`  ⚠️ Final LLM verification skipped`)
+    }
+  }
+
+  // Cleanup
+  console.log(`  🧹 Cleaning up namespace ${namespace}...`)
+  if (!DRY_RUN) {
+    execCommand(`kubectl delete namespace ${namespace} --wait=false 2>/dev/null || true`)
+  }
+
+  report.duration_ms = Date.now() - startTime
+
+  if (allPassed && verificationPassed) {
+    report.verdict = 'pass'
+    console.log(`\n  ✅ VERDICT: PASS (${(report.duration_ms / 1000).toFixed(1)}s)`)
+  } else if (allPassed) {
+    report.verdict = 'pass_unverified'
+    console.log(`\n  ⚠️ VERDICT: PASS (unverified — no verification step confirmed)`)
+  } else if (verificationPassed) {
+    report.verdict = 'partial'
+    console.log(`\n  ⚠️ VERDICT: PARTIAL (some steps failed but verification passed)`)
+  } else {
+    report.verdict = 'fail'
+    console.log(`\n  ❌ VERDICT: FAIL (${(report.duration_ms / 1000).toFixed(1)}s)`)
+  }
+
+  return report
+}
+
+// ── Main ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+async function main() {
+  const files = process.argv.slice(2)
+  if (files.length === 0) {
+    console.error('Usage: node mission-executor.mjs <mission.json> [mission2.json ...]')
+    process.exit(1)
+  }
+
+  // Verify cluster connectivity
+  console.log('🔌 Checking cluster connectivity...')
+  const clusterCheck = execCommand('kubectl cluster-info --request-timeout=10s 2>&1 | head -3')
+  if (!clusterCheck.success) {
+    console.error('❌ Cannot connect to cluster:', clusterCheck.output)
+    process.exit(1)
+  }
+  console.log(`✅ Connected: ${clusterCheck.output.split('\n')[0]}`)
+
+  // Verify helm is available
+  const helmCheck = execCommand('helm version --short 2>/dev/null')
+  console.log(`✅ Helm: ${helmCheck.success ? helmCheck.output : 'not available'}`)
+
+  const results = []
+  for (const file of files) {
+    try {
+      const report = await executeMission(file)
+      results.push(report)
+    } catch (err) {
+      console.error(`\n❌ Fatal error executing ${file}: ${err.message}`)
+      results.push({ mission: file, verdict: 'error', error: err.message })
+    }
+  }
+
+  // Summary
+  console.log(`\n${'═'.repeat(70)}`)
+  console.log('  📊 Execution Summary')
+  console.log(`${'═'.repeat(70)}`)
+
+  const passed = results.filter(r => r.verdict === 'pass').length
+  const partial = results.filter(r => r.verdict === 'partial' || r.verdict === 'pass_unverified').length
+  const failed = results.filter(r => r.verdict === 'fail' || r.verdict === 'error').length
+
+  for (const r of results) {
+    const icon = { pass: '✅', partial: '⚠️', pass_unverified: '⚠️', fail: '❌', error: '💥' }[r.verdict] || '❓'
+    const dur = r.duration_ms ? ` (${(r.duration_ms / 1000).toFixed(1)}s)` : ''
+    console.log(`  ${icon} ${r.mission}${dur} — ${r.verdict}`)
+  }
+
+  console.log(`\n  Total: ${results.length} | ✅ ${passed} | ⚠️ ${partial} | ❌ ${failed}`)
+
+  // Write report
+  const reportPath = process.env.REPORT_PATH || 'mission-execution-report.json'
+  writeFileSync(reportPath, JSON.stringify({ timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), results, summary: { total: results.length, passed, partial, failed } }, null, 2))
+  console.log(`\n  📝 Report: ${reportPath}`)
+
+  // Exit code: fail if >50% of missions failed
+  if (failed > results.length / 2) {
+    console.log('\n  ❌ Majority of missions failed — failing CI')
+    process.exit(1)
+  }
+}
+
+main().catch(err => {
+  console.error('Fatal:', err)
+  process.exit(1)
+})
